prompt:
Design a LeaseHub Operations Console to manage multi-tenant coworking lease operations, on-site customer feedback, and compliance reporting in a fully offline, enterprise-grade desktop application. The primary users are System Administrator (creates tenants, sets security policies), Tenant Admin (manages a single location or portfolio), Operations Manager (daily occupancy and order oversight), Compliance Auditor (read-only audit access), and Content Moderator (review governance). The UI runs on Windows 11 with an English interface and is optimized for 1920x1080 and above with high-DPI scaling; it supports multi-window workflows where a manager can keep a live dashboard window alongside a contract workspace and an audit log viewer. Desktop interactions must be keyboard-first with configurable shortcuts (e.g., Ctrl+K global search, Ctrl+E export, Ctrl+Shift+L open audit log), right-click context menus for approve/reject/edit actions, deep clipboard copy for table cells, and a system tray mode that keeps scheduled reports running while minimizing to tray. Dashboards provide local analytics for orders, revenue, occupancy rate, hottest time slots/seats, cancellation rate, and repurchase rate, with filters by store, date range, and hour-of-day; exports generate CSV and PDF to a user-selected folder. Scheduled operations reports run daily at 6:00 AM local time and weekly every Monday at 7:00 AM, writing immutable report snapshots. Reviews support 1–5 star ratings, text up to 2,000 characters, and up to 5 locally stored images (JPG/PNG, max 5 MB each), with follow-up reviews within 14 days and merchant replies within 7 days; moderation applies a configurable sensitive-word dictionary and policy flags, while basic anti-fraud rules quarantine reviews from the same local account posting more than 3 reviews in 10 minutes or repeating identical text across locations.

All data is persisted locally using SQLite, with Dear ImGui providing a responsive desktop UI layer; no network calls, third-party logins, email/SMS verification, or external map services are permitted. The core data model includes Tenant, OrgUnit (department/position), User, Role, Permission (menu/API/field/resource), DataScope rules (extensible ABAC conditions like locationId and departmentId), Order, Seat/Room, OccupancySnapshot, Review, ReviewAsset, ContractTemplate, ContractInstance, and AuditEvent. E-contract workflows use template-driven parameterization (e.g., lease term 1–36 months, monthly rent $500.00–$50,000.00, payment cycle monthly/quarterly, default clause catalog), version every edit, and archive final PDFs to the local file system; identity verification is offline via re-authentication (password re-entry) plus a locally managed signer roster requiring a stored government ID last-4 field and an admin-approved “Verified” flag. Expiration reminders are local notifications inside the app and tray badges starting 60/30/7 days before end date, driving renewal or move-out checklists. Audit and compliance require append-only event writing with a hash chain per tenant (SHA-256), 7-year retention, and tamper-evident verification reports; auditors can search by user, object, action, and time, then export signed audit bundles (ZIP containing CSV/PDF plus hash manifest). Route planning and ETA are supported for offline field tasks (e.g., property walk-throughs or maintenance runs) using manually entered addresses and a locally stored road network dataset imported from a USB drive; the engine optimizes up to 25 stops, estimates mileage/time/tolls using configurable per-mile rates, and supports versioned detour/restricted-road rules with one-click strategy switching and rollback. Performance targets include cold start under 3 seconds on a typical business laptop, steady-state memory under 300 MB, and long-running stability with explicit resource release for image buffers and database statements; crashes must trigger automatic state recovery via 60-second checkpoints and a “Restore last session” prompt. Updates are handled by offline package import (signed update file), with an admin-controlled rollback to the prior version.
